title: react-loading-skeleton npm — 1M+ Weekly Downloads
insights: 1M+ weekly downloads. SkeletonTheme provider. Wave and pulse animations. Automatically sizes based on surrounding text. Widely used as standard skeleton approach in React. Alternative: Tailwind CSS animate-pulse class (no extra dependency).

title: cmdk npm Package — Install Stats
insights: 1.5M+ weekly downloads. Used by shadcn command component, Vercel, Linear, Raycast. Solid adoption signal for command palette pattern.

title: React Aria Autocomplete — New Alpha Component 2025
insights: New in Jan 2025 release (alpha). Unlike Combobox, Autocomplete allows free-form text with optional suggestions. Enables Searchable Menus, Searchable Selects, Command Palettes. Critical pattern for HR: live candidate search, job title autocomplete.

title: React Aria ColorPicker — Full Suite
insights: Complete color picker system: ColorArea (2D hue/saturation), ColorSlider (channel), ColorWheel (hue circle), ColorField (hex/channel text input), ColorSwatch (preview), ColorSwatchPicker (palette). Most complete accessible color picker in any React library.

title: Chakra UI v3 Announcement — Architecture Changes
insights: Open compound components, recipe-based variants (Panda CSS inspired), CSS animations (no framer-motion), RSC support, Ark UI foundation. Unification with Park UI ecosystem. 25+ new components added. Migration guide from v2.

title: chakra-ui/ark GitHub — 3k+ Stars
insights: 3k+ stars. 45+ components on Zag.js state machines. Framework parity: React, Vue, Solid, Svelte. Unique components: AngleSlider, ColorPicker, DatePicker, FileUpload, NumberInput, PinInput, RatingGroup, Signature Pad, Timer, Tour. Most comprehensive headless library.

title: Zag.js Introduction — State Machine Approach
insights: Each component is a finite state machine with defined states and transitions. No invalid states possible. Fully tested behavior. Framework adapters bridge machines to React/Vue/Solid. Pre-built machines: accordion, combobox, date-picker, dialog, file-upload, hover-card, menu, pagination, popover, progress, radio-group, range-slider, rating-group, select, slider, splitter, tabs, tags-input, timer, toggle, tooltip.

title: Material UI v6 — 100+ Material Design Components
insights: v6 stable with Pigment CSS (zero-runtime CSS-in-JS), CSS Variables via cssVariables flag, Grid v2 with CSS gap, React 19 support. 100+ components. v7 planned H1 2025 with ESM improvements. @mui/x for advanced: DataGrid, DatePickers, Charts, TreeView. Most used React component library globally.

title: MUI X TimePicker — Desktop and Mobile Variants
insights: DesktopTimePicker, MobileTimePicker, TimePicker (adaptive). 12h/24h format. Minutes, hours, seconds. Timezone support via adapter. Controlled + uncontrolled. Works inside forms with React Hook Form. Premium tier but widely licensed.

title: Top React Date Pickers Comparison — LogRocket
insights: Covers react-datepicker, react-day-picker, MUI X, Mantine, react-date-range (unmaintained). react-datepicker: 7M+ downloads/week, feature-rich. react-day-picker: WCAG AA, shadcn default calendar. Recommendation: react-day-picker for accessibility-focused projects.

title: 20 React Datepickers Ranked 2026
insights: Comprehensive ranking methodology. Tests: mobile UX, timezone, range selection, disabled dates, localization, a11y. Top picks: react-datepicker (maintenance), react-day-picker (accessibility), MUI X (enterprise). Filters out unmaintained libraries.

title: React Aria useSlider — Accessible Range Slider
insights: useSlider + useSliderThumb hooks. ARIA slider role with aria-valuetext. Keyboard: arrow keys, Page Up/Down, Home/End. Multi-thumb support for ranges. Works with any styling. More correct than most slider libraries regarding ARIA spec.

title: Radix UI Slider — WAI-ARIA Compliant Range
insights: Follows WAI-ARIA Slider design pattern. Keyboard navigation: ArrowLeft/Right/Up/Down, Home/End. Multiple thumbs. Step, min, max, inverted props. CSS custom properties for styling. Used in shadcn/ui Slider component.

title: react-range — Accessible Range with Custom Marks
insights: Accessible range input following ARIA patterns. Render props for full customization. Multiple thumbs support. Custom marks and labels. Works independently of any styling framework. Good alternative when Radix/Aria is too heavy.

title: shadcn/ui Sidebar — Composable Navigation System
insights: Full composable sidebar system: SidebarProvider, Sidebar, SidebarHeader, SidebarContent, SidebarGroup, SidebarMenu, SidebarMenuItem, SidebarMenuButton, SidebarTrigger, SidebarInset. useSidebar hook for state. Mobile-responsive with Sheet fallback. Critical for HR dashboard layout.

title: shadcn/ui Data Table — Official Guide with TanStack
insights: Official shadcn data table guide. Combines Table (HTML) + TanStack Table (logic). Covers: column definitions, sorting, filtering, pagination, row selection, column visibility. Foundation pattern for all data-heavy views in the project.

title: shadcn/ui Drawer — Built on Vaul
insights: Uses Vaul. Bottom drawer with snap points. Swipe-to-dismiss. Mobile-first replacement for Dialog on small screens. Same API as Dialog but slides up from bottom. Essential for mobile HR workflows: candidate quick view, job detail sheet.

title: shadcn/ui Calendar — react-day-picker Based
insights: Built on react-day-picker. Composable Calendar component. Accepts timeZone prop. Used inside shadcn/ui DatePicker (Calendar + Popover). Multiple/range selection modes. shadcn wraps react-day-picker in its own styled component you own.

title: shadcn/ui CLI 3.0 and MCP Server — August 2025
insights: CLI 3.0: namespaced registries (@registry/name format), view before install, search, list commands. MCP server for AI agents to discover and install components. Registry resolution engine rewritten. Direct EAIP relevance: MCP server enables automated component scaffolding.

title: shadcn/ui npx shadcn create — December 2025
insights: npx shadcn create: pick component library, icons, base color, theme, fonts. 5 new visual styles. Customization at project creation time. Marks evolution from fixed-style library to configurable starter.

title: Makers Den — React UI Libraries Comparison Matrix 2025
insights: Side-by-side: bundle sizes, TypeScript quality, RSC support, dark mode, theming. shadcn: no bundle (owns code), Mantine: 40-60KB, MUI: 90KB+, Chakra: 50KB. RSC: shadcn/HeroUI/Base UI — yes, Mantine/MUI — partial, Chakra v2 — no (v3 yes).

title: shadcn vs Radix vs Tailwind UI — Choice Guide 2025
insights: Tailwind UI = paid ($299). shadcn = free copy-paste. Radix = headless. Recommendation: shadcn for full-stack apps, Radix when building your own design system from scratch, Tailwind UI for premium pre-built sections.

title: Ant Design vs shadcn/ui — 2025 Comparison
insights: antd: 60+ components, enterprise patterns, dense data tables, Alibaba-tested. shadcn: Tailwind-based, full code ownership, no breaking changes. antd for data-heavy enterprise (ERP, finance). shadcn for custom SaaS. Both valid for RH-OptimERP — different layers possible.

title: 10 Best shadcn Alternatives 2025
insights: Park UI (Panda CSS), Aceternity UI (animations), Magic UI (effects), Origin UI, Syntax UI, tremor. Shadow Panda (Panda CSS + Radix). Animata. Lists ecosystem alternatives for when shadcn defaults don't fit.

title: Headless vs WYSIWYG Editors 2025 — Architecture Landscape
insights: Headless: TipTap, Lexical, Slate, Plate, ProseMirror — full control, build your UI. WYSIWYG: BlockNote, TinyMCE, Quill — ready UI, less control. For HR (job descriptions, candidate notes): TipTap headless with custom toolbar is the elite pattern.

title: TipTap vs Lexical — Architecture Comparison
insights: TipTap: ProseMirror-based, best DX, extensions for mentions/tables/code/collab. Lexical: Meta, faster DOM updates, smaller core, newer. Lexical better for performance-critical apps. TipTap better for feature-rich editors with collaboration.

title: Best React Select Component Libraries — LogRocket 2025
insights: react-select (27k stars, async, creatable), Downshift (WAI-ARIA primitives), Choices.js (lightweight 20KB), react-selectize (tags + AJAX). Each for different use case. React-select for complex multi-select with async, Downshift when building custom UI.

title: Top 5 React Drag-and-Drop Libraries 2025
insights: dnd-kit (10KB, WCAG 2.1 AA), hello-pangea/dnd (react-beautiful-dnd fork, maintained), pragmatic-drag-and-drop (Atlassian), react-dnd (lower level), react-sortable-hoc (deprecated). dnd-kit is the clear winner for new projects.

title: Popper vs Floating UI — Migration Guide
insights: Floating UI is the evolution of Popper 2 by the same author. Same positioning engine, smaller footprint, better React hooks API. Supports React Native and Canvas (Popper was DOM-only). All major libraries (Radix, MUI, Tippy.js) have migrated to Floating UI.

title: ARIAKit at React Advanced 2025 — WCAG Without Expertise
insights: Aurora Scharff's React Advanced 2025 talk. Ariakit enables WCAG-compliant custom components without deep a11y expertise. React 19 integration demo. WCAG 2 legally mandatory in EU from June 28, 2025. Key compliance driver for HR software.

title: React Accessibility Best Practices — WCAG 2.2 Guide
insights: WCAG 2.2 AA patterns for React. Tools: eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y, @axe-core/react, @testing-library/jest-dom. Focus management patterns. ARIA live regions for dynamic updates. Dynamic React updates can break ARIA states — requires key prop strategy.

title: Motion — JavaScript and React Animation Library Homepage
insights: motion/react import. 120fps via Web Animations API. AnimatePresence for exit animations. LayoutAnimation for shared element transitions. Scroll-linked animations. 330+ pre-built snippets. v12 with React 19 support and OKLCH colors.

title: CSS Variables and Design Tokens Guide 2025
insights: Design tokens as CSS variables are the 2025 standard. Primitive tokens (--blue-500) vs semantic tokens (--color-brand-primary). Runtime theming without recompilation. Dark mode via prefers-color-scheme or class toggle. React Context + CSS vars = theme change without re-renders.

title: tremorlabs/tremor GitHub — 16k+ Stars
insights: 16k+ stars. Vercel-owned. Copy-paste like shadcn. 35+ components + 300 blocks. Components: AreaChart, BarChart, LineChart, DonutChart, ScatterChart, SparkAreaChart, SparkBarChart, BarList, Tracker, ProgressBar, KPICard, Badge, Bold, Table, MultiSelect, DateRangePicker.

title: Drag-and-Drop Libraries npm Comparison
insights: @dnd-kit/core: 1.2M+ weekly downloads, growing. react-beautiful-dnd: declining (archived). react-dnd: steady legacy users. react-sortable-hoc: deprecated. Download trends confirm dnd-kit as the winner for new projects.

title: react-input-mask — Phone and Date Masking
insights: 585k weekly downloads. 2.3k stars. Mask string approach: 9=digit, a=letter, *=alphanumeric. Phone: +1 (999) 999-9999. French phone: +33 (0)9 99 99 99 99. IE8+ compatible. Best for simple fixed masks; for dynamic masks use @react-input/mask.

title: react-phone-number-input — International Phone Input
insights: International phone number input with country flag selector. libphonenumber-js validation. Country selector with search. Supports all locales. Critical for multinational HR: French phone (+33) and international candidate contacts.

title: react-timezone-select — Timezone Selector Component
insights: DST-aware timezone selector. Built on react-select. Search by city or timezone name. Auto-detects user timezone. Companion for time pickers in scheduling modules. Key for HR scheduling across time zones.

title: react-window vs react-virtuoso — Virtualization Comparison
insights: react-window (bvaughn): lightweight, fixed and variable sizes. react-virtuoso: dynamic heights, grouping, sticky headers built-in, infinite scroll. react-virtuoso has better DX for complex lists. @tanstack/react-virtual: most composable, used with TanStack Table.

title: 3 Ways to Implement Infinite Scroll in React
insights: IntersectionObserver API (recommended 2025), react-infinite-scroll-component, react-window + onScroll. IntersectionObserver: no scroll event listeners, efficient, modern browser support. Pair with TanStack Query useInfiniteQuery for server-side data.

title: Infinite Scrolling in React — IntersectionObserver Approach
insights: IntersectionObserver step-by-step. useRef for last element observation. useEffect for observer lifecycle. Watches DOM, not scroll position. Standard pattern used by Twitter, YouTube, LinkedIn feeds. Candidate list pagination pattern.

title: HeroUI Pagination — Accessible React Aria Based
insights: Built on React Aria Button. nav[aria-label=pagination] + role=navigation. aria-current=page on active. focus rings via :focus-visible. Tab keyboard navigation. Compact variant for mobile. Loop prop for cyclic navigation.

title: MUI Pagination — Standard Enterprise Pagination
insights: Controlled + uncontrolled. count, page, onChange. Compact variant. Rounded variant. Size: small/medium/large. usePagination hook for custom UI. Screen reader: aria-label per page, nav wrapper. Works seamlessly with MUI DataGrid.

title: Badges vs Chips vs Tags vs Pills — Design Pattern Guide
insights: Definitional clarity: Badges=static status indicators, Tags=static/interactive labels, Chips=interactive selections/filters, Pills=rounded badges. Elite design systems are explicit: don't mix static and interactive visual styles. 48px minimum touch target. 8px spacing between interactive elements.

title: Base UI Combobox — MUI Headless Combobox
insights: Unstyled combobox from MUI Base UI v1. Named slots: Root, Input, Popup, Listbox, Option. Full keyboard navigation: arrow keys, Home/End, Escape. Filter logic included. Better than Downshift API per InfoQ analysis. Key component for search-as-you-type HR workflows.

title: Base UI Context Menu — Right-Click Menu
insights: Context menu on right-click or long press. Not available in Radix Primitives (gap). Base UI v1 fills this gap. Named slots: Root, Trigger, Portal, Positioner, Popup, Item, Group, Separator. Important for candidate table actions (right-click to edit/delete/view).

title: Base UI Autocomplete — Free-form Text with Suggestions
insights: Unlike Combobox, Autocomplete allows free-form text (suggestions are optional). Named slots: Input, Popup, Option. Keyboard completion. Good for skills autocomplete in candidate profiles where new values are allowed.

title: better-cmdk — AI-Powered Command Palette
insights: AI chat + action approvals + fuzzy search in one command menu. Extends cmdk concept with AI integration. Hosted AI chat endpoint (free trial). Pattern for AI-assisted HR workflows: natural language candidate search, AI-powered job matching via command interface.

title: react-timeline-gantt — Virtualized Gantt/Timeline
insights: Virtual rendering Gantt chart. Handles large datasets via virtualization. Task bars, dependencies, zoom levels. Open source. Lighter alternative to enterprise Gantt libraries. Good for recruitment pipeline timeline view.

title: lightGallery — React Image Gallery and Carousel
insights: React component for image/video gallery with lightbox. Pinch-to-zoom, rotate, flip, social sharing, animated thumbnails. Touch and keyboard navigation. Responsive. For HR: candidate profile photos, document previews.

title: React Spinners — 25+ Spinner Variants
insights: 25+ spinner types: ClipLoader, BeatLoader, BarLoader, BounceLoader, etc. Color, size, loading props. Lightweight. CSS-based animations. Standard loading indicator for async operations in HR dashboard.

title: Page Transitions in React — Motion AnimatePresence
insights: AnimatePresence enables exit animations before component unmount. Route transitions with React Router. Fade, slide, scale patterns. Key pattern: route-level AnimatePresence with motion.div wrapper. Elevates perceived performance in HR multi-step flows.

title: Fluent UI React Storybook — Full Component Catalog
insights: Interactive catalog of all Fluent UI React v9 components. Live prop editing. Shows Microsoft Teams-scale component usage. DataGrid with cell navigation, row selection, multi-sort. SpinButton (accessible number input). TeachingPopover for onboarding flows.

title: Best React Carousel Libraries 2025 — Ranked
insights: Embla (lightweight, shadcn-default), Swiper (feature-rich, 34k stars), Keen-Slider (40KB, SSR), React Slick (legacy but popular), React Multi Carousel. Swiper recommended for full-featured, Embla for minimal/custom.

title: react-colorful npm — 1.5M+ Weekly Downloads
insights: 1.5M+ weekly downloads. 2.8KB gzipped. Most popular color picker for modern React. Confirmation that tiny, focused, accessible libraries win download share over monolithic solutions.

title: @dnd-kit/core npm — Install Stats and Changelog
insights: 1.2M+ weekly downloads. Steady growth. MIT license. Packages: @dnd-kit/core, @dnd-kit/sortable, @dnd-kit/utilities, @dnd-kit/modifiers. Active maintenance.

title: shadcn vs MUI vs Ant Design — Complete Comparison 2026
insights: Three-way comparison. shadcn: no bundle, full code ownership, Tailwind-native, best for custom SaaS. MUI: 90KB+, Material Design, largest ecosystem, MUI X for advanced. antd: 60+ components, enterprise-proven, Alibaba-scale, best data tables free. Each excels in different context.

title: shadcn vs Radix — Subframe Analysis
insights: shadcn for speed with flexibility. Radix when building pure design system from scratch. For most product teams, shadcn gives the right balance. Key: shadcn uses Radix internally, so you get Radix quality without Radix complexity.

title: Best Multiple Select Components React 2026 — Ranked
insights: 10 options ranked: react-select (most features), Downshift (most accessible), MUI Autocomplete (Material), shadcn/ui Combobox (copy-paste), HeroUI TagGroup (new Dec 2025). For skill multi-select in HR: react-select with createable for new skills.

title: Top 5 React Drag-and-Drop 2026 — Puck Editor Blog
insights: 2026 update: dnd-kit still #1. pragmatic-drag-and-drop by Atlassian as #2 (smaller bundle). hello-pangea/dnd for lists-only use cases. react-dnd for complex tree drag. Puck editor itself uses dnd-kit for page builder drag.

title: TanStack Table — Virtualized Columns Example
insights: Horizontal column virtualization. Different strategy than row virtualization: needs column offsets. Uses columnVirtualizer with horizontal scroll. Essential for wide HR tables: many candidate attributes visible simultaneously.

title: Virtualized Table: TanStack Virtual + React Query + shadcn
insights: Full recipe: TanStack Virtual + TanStack Query + shadcn/ui Table components. useVirtualizer for rows, useInfiniteQuery for data fetching. Shows the complete pattern for enterprise candidate lists with 10k+ records and server-side pagination.
